title: Cut December 2014 Interview with Sadamoto
wikitext:
[]: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Apr 02, 2019 4:57 pm So I have found this in the Pixiv wiki, in the Kawoshin and in the Asuka pages, both in the sections concerning Sadamoto's manga: Cut2014年12月号のインタビューで貞本氏は 「カヲルはシンジのことを一番わかってくれている存在なんですよね。わかっているが故に、僕のことを本当に好きだったら殺して欲しいと、矛盾に満ちた踏み絵を突きつけるっていうね」「現実的にシンジが“人を好きになる”ことと向き合うエピソードで、カヲルを存在させたっていうことです」とコメントしている。

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 02, 2019 5:13 pm FelipeFritschF wrote: Do notice the "no romantic meaning" is not a quote from the translation but rather inserted by the wiki.

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Apr 02, 2019 5:21 pm Reichu wrote: It's a paraphrase. と発言している = [according to the interview Sadamoto] says that(...).

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Apr 02, 2019 5:41 pm Oooh, found this in the Reishin page now: 漫画版ではより二人の関係や「手を繋ぐこと」に焦点が当てられている。また、「レイとシンジの想いは男女間の恋愛」、両想いであるとCut2014年12月号の作者インタビューで言及されている。

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jun 06, 2019 6:11 pm I've been telling ppl that its canon since the proposal sketch. That said Sadamoto isn't always unambiguous on this like right after the finale came out there was an interview where he seemed to go with the semi-maternal interpretation. Ovsly that doesn't mean that Asuka's and Shinji's reincarnations couldn't be involved later just that they aren't yet, and like the point with the "hands" as a motif for the many kinds of encounters one can have and hence that this scene shows how instrumentality was averted and ppl continue to interact. What's more relevant is that he confirmed that they are indeed reincarnations or at least "different versions" It would be really nice to have the actual quotes with proper grammar (esp the Kaworu oneLike is he implying sadism or just insight?) so as to not take other ppls word for it/ form ones own opinion properly.

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jul 21, 2020 7:28 pm Cut2014年12月号のインタビューで貞本氏は 「カヲルはシンジのことを一番わかってくれている存在なんですよね。わかっているが故に、僕のことを本当に好きだったら殺して欲しいと、矛盾に満ちた踏み絵を突きつけるっていうね」「現実的にシンジが“人を好きになる”ことと向き合うエピソードで、カヲルを存在させたっていうことです」とコメントしている。 In December 2014 Cut Magazine interview Sadamoto makes the following comments: "Kaworu is the one who understands Shinji the best of them all. Although he understands, Kaworu consequently thrusts a contradictory fumi-e at Shinji by asking Shinji to kill him if he really loves him." "To make Shinji confront in reality a situation in which he 'comes to love a person' Kaworu was made to exist." Cut (カット) 2014年 12月号の貞本エヴァインタビューによると「最終回に出てくるアスカは、これまでの本編でのアスカではなくて、次の新しい世界でも知りあえるであろう魅力的な女性の象徴として出している」とのことで、シンジとの恋愛的な意味は無いと発言している。 Going by Sadamoto's interview in December 2014 Cut Magazine "Asuka that appears in final chapter is not same Asuka who has appeared so far, she appears as a symbol of attractive female who Shinji is sure to meet in the next world too" is how things stand, Sadamoto claims the ending implies nothing romantic regarding her tie with Shinji. 漫画版ではより二人の関係や「手を繋ぐこと」に焦点が当てられている。また、「レイとシンジの想いは男女間の恋愛」、両想いであるとCut2014年12月号の作者インタビューで言及されている。 In manga version the relationship between the two and "handholding" are brought into focus. In addition, "Rei and Shinji's feelings are those of love between man and woman", Sadamoto affirms in December 2014 Cut Magazine interview their love is mutual.

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jul 21, 2020 11:08 pm Xard's translation is on point, though the snippets up on the shipping pixiv entries (via the Cut magazine interview—I vaguely remember hearing about that when it first came out, too) just go to show my personal preference of Anno's Eva. For one, Shinji's got mixed up feelings for all 3 ladies (at least initially before things go all to hell). Also it's for me to not be a little regarding Asuka in the final chapter isn't the manga Asuka, simply reincarnated—what would that make of Shinji and Kensuke, in this "new world", then? Especially how Shinji (still) has Misato's necklace with him and the fossilized MP Evas....

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Tue Jul 21, 2020 11:19 pm Though that's how I always interpreted it, unfortunately just makes Asuka in the ending feel more meaningless. I understand Sadamoto might be going for a purely thematic ending, but that essentially makes the actual story and characters meaningless because "their" world ended as did their existence, without resulting in anything. In EoE, Shinji's story didn't end, only a part of it, and the rest was just beginning. In the manga, he might as well be Simon because we're talking about essentially an entirely different character that didn't go through Shinji's trials. Sadamoto is such a hack. :/

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 22, 2020 11:44 am Yeah, "meaningless" is exactly the issue I take with it. I have no problem with the underlying idea of "Lilin/the Earth reincarnated post-3I*, memory wipe and all"...It's the execution of it that leaves much to be desired. I mean, what's the point of manga Shinji's journey, then...? *And if that's not the point that Lilin can find happiness now, then WTF is ? Though I never really enjoyed the Eva manga, I'm venting out of the frustration that it could have been something really special, since some of the "new" story elements (like Kaji's backstory, or Shinji grieving for Toji actually dying) were quite interesting.

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Jul 22, 2020 4:10 pm Sadamoto is an amazing illustrator, one of the best in the whole industry, and I think he should stick to that...

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 22, 2020 5:56 pm He definitely should stick to illustrations since his art's seriously excellent, but it's a shame he couldn't keep his extreme views, uh, private since it left a lot of understandably angry fans.

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Jul 25, 2020 1:54 am I might have liked, or at least respected the change of Manga!Shinji vs NGE!Shinji, if Sadamoto had gone full Natsuki Subaru with him, and made his flaws a clear result of his anti-social habits/upbringing. Something he needed to overcome, just in a different vein from NGE. As presented, he just feels closer to a typical hot-headed shounen protagonist. Things are different because they needed to be, I get that. But the writer still has a duty to make it interesting. I'm looking at you, Digimon 2020 .

[]: Re: "Cut December 2014" Interview with Sadamoto             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jul 28, 2020 6:21 am I've been rereading though Sadamoto's Eva, just because it's different from NGE and NTE, I haven't revisited it as often as the Anno Eva, and because it's sightly different and I've forgotten a lot of the unique narrative touches. (Seeing Rei pilot Unit 01 in the first chapters, for example, was still shocking on the second time around.) But I do remember the ending feeling a little.... "deflated" on my first reading. Still, I'll continue to reread it just to have some marginally fresher Eva stories in front of my eyes that don't devolve into complete fan fic aesthetics. (Most of what I hear about Shinji Ikari Rising Project sounds really fun, even if in a fan-fic-y sort of way, but is definitely not what I'm in the mood for with the Shin Eva delay. Sadamoto's Eva, at the very least, is a bit closer to that, even if it still misses the mark considerably in a few ways.) And, yeah, Sadamoto's more xenophobic views can go jump in a fire. I adore his character designs whenever I see them, but I'm not a fan of watching Summer Wars or Wolf Children and being subconsciously reminded, "Oh yeah, every one of these characters is designed by a racist, or at least by someone who acts like one online."

Article: Cut December 2014 Interview with Sadamoto
 as the Anno Eva, and because it's sightly different and I've forgotten a lot of the unique narrative touches. (Seeing Rei pilot Unit 01 in the first chapters, for example, was still shocking on the second time around.) But I do remember the ending feeling a little.... "deflated" on my first reading. Still, I'll continue to reread it just to have some marginally fresher Eva stories in front of my eyes that don't devolve into complete fan fic aesthetics. (Most of what I hear about Shinji Ikari Rising Project sounds really fun, even if in a fan-fic-y sort of way, but is definitely not what I'm in the mood for with the Shin Eva delay. Sadamoto's Eva, at the very least, is a bit closer to that, even if it still misses the mark considerably in a few ways.) And, yeah, Sadamoto's more xenophobic views can go jump in a fire. I adore his character designs whenever I see them, but I'm not a fan of watching Summer Wars or Wolf Children and being subconsciously reminded, "Oh yeah, every one of these characters is designed by a racist, or at least by someone who acts like one online."
